data.title: Taiwan's Inflation Slows to 1.55% in May
data.description:
Taiwan's consumer price index rose 1.55% annually in May, but slid by 0.27% from the preceding month, Taiwan's Directorate General of Budget, Accounting and Statistics disclosed Thursday.

The monthly softening in inflation is attributable to a 5.25%, 3.72% and 1.55% drop in the indices for fuels and lubricants, vegetables as well as garments, respectively.

Annually, inflation rose 18.56% for the index of fruits, 4.80% for meats and 3.50% for food away from home, it said.

Core inflation, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, rose 1.61% versus the year earlier, the data showed.

FORM 8.5 (EPT/RI)

PUBLIC DEALING DISCLOSURE BY AN EXEMPT PRINCIPAL TRADER WITH

Where there have been dealings in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(b), copy table 2(a), (b), (c) or (d) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security dealt in.

3. OTHER INFORMATION

(a) Indemnity and other dealing arrangements

  • Details of any indemnity or option arrangement, or any agreement or understanding, formal or informal, relating to relevant securities which may be an inducement to deal or refrain from dealing entered into by the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any party to the offer or any person acting in concert with a party to the offer:

     Irrevocable commitments and letters of intent should not be included. If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

  • None

(b) Agreements, arrangements or understandings relating to options or derivatives

  • Details of any agreement, arrangement or understanding, formal or informal, between the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any other person relating to:

     (i) the voting rights of any relevant securities under any option; or

     (ii) the voting rights or future acquisition or disposal of any relevant securities to which any derivative is referenced:

  • None

data.title: United Microelectronics Posts Lower Net Sales in May
data.description:
United Microelectronics said Thursday its net sales were 19.48 billion New Taiwan dollars ($650.6 million) in May, down 0.2% from a year earlier.

For the five months through May, the company's net sales rose 4.2% year over year to NT$97.79 billion.

data.title: Stablecoin Issuer Circle Internet Group Prices Upsized $1.05 Billion IPO
data.description:
Circle Internet Group (CRCL), a global financial technology company that's behind the USDC stablecoin, said late Wednesday it priced an upsized initial public offering of 34 million class A common shares at $31 each.

Circle is offering 14.8 million of the shares, with the rest offered by certain selling stockholders.

The company said it has granted a 30-day overallotment option to the underwriters to acquire up to 5.1 million additional shares.

Circle expects its shares to start trading on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol CRCL Thursday, while the IPO is expected to close Friday.

data.title: Kepler Cheuvreux cuts Bankinter as valuation catches up with fundamentals
data.description:
** Kepler Cheuvreux cuts Bankinter to "hold" from "buy," citing limited share price upside in the near term

** It points out the Spanish bank continues to deliver, with rising fee income, tight cost control, and returns well above its peers, but much of the strength is already reflected in the price

** It notes that shares trade at about 1.7x forward tangible book, more than two standard deviations above their long-term average

** "We still like the business and its long-term outlook but see the current risk-reward as more balanced," it adds

** Up to the previous session's close, the stock gained 46%​ YTD

** Out of 21 analysts covering Bankinter, eight it "strong buy"/"buy,"​ seven "hold" and six​ "strong sell"/"sell" - LSEG data

data.title: Intrum Comments On Investigation Into Intrum Sweden
data.description:

data.title: Strategic merger: Agelec joins the Videlio group
data.description:
The growing challenges of supervision, decision-making efficiency, and security for institutions and businesses have led Videlio, a major player in audiovisual and IT integration, to take a step forward in the development of a new strategic activity: control systems. This turning point is now taking place through a major merger with Agelec, the French leader in supervision and control solutions for sensitive infrastructure. 

This marks a defining moment for the Videlio group, confirming its ambition to offer a wider range of solutions that are among the most innovative on the market. The Group's goal is to stay as close as possible to its customers' operational needs and uses by offering them solutions that are easy to use, secure, and effective.

Industrial synergy between two leading players

AGELEC stands out today as the specialist in supervision, control, and visualization solutions dedicated to critical environments, meeting the constant demand for availability and security of sensitive infrastructure. Its control or supervision rooms, security stations, crisis rooms, and command or surveillance centers demonstrate Agelec's recognized experience in meeting these very high standards. This expertise has attracted leading clients.

Videlio and Agelec will work together to strengthen this expertise by combining their operational and commercial resources to give new scope to this activity within the Group. Their ambition is to develop a model capable of combining the best technologies in open architectures that guarantee both security and interoperability.  

This project will focus in particular on the development of advanced collaborative incident management solutions, agile and robust architectures with AVoIP, and intelligent video walls. Videlio and Agelec will also work together on today's major challenges, with innovative offerings in AI, cybersecurity, augmented reality, and more.

Xavier Renaud, President of Videlio:

“Agelec is a leader in control rooms, particularly through its mastery of the technological and operational complexities of this type of installation. We are very pleased with this merger, which strengthens Videlio's position as the French leader in specialized high value-added audiovisual integration, while expanding our scope of activity in a market in which we strongly believe. We enthusiastically welcome the Agelec teams and their leader Guillaume Guérin, who has built a solid, innovative company that is virtually indispensable in its ecosystem."

Guillaume Guérin, President of Agelec:

“The energy and human values that drive Videlio were decisive factors in our choice. We share the same vision of audiovisual integration, which brings high standards and value creation for the end customer. Our common conviction is to place usability, ergonomics, and business intelligence above technology. Joining Videlio is obviously an opportunity to take a step forward, but above all, it is an opportunity to amplify our innovation momentum in intelligent and open systems, to better support our customers as they face the constant evolution of their critical challenges."

data.title: RailTel Corporation of India Gets Letter of Intent for Order Worth INR2.74 Billion; Shares Up 3%
data.description:
RailTel Corporation of India has received a letter of intent for an order worth about 2.74 billion Indian rupees from the Motor Vehicles Department, Maharashtra, India, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The contract is for the designing, implementation, operation, and maintainance of an intelligent traffic management system on various blackspots and vulnerable spots in the Vidarbha Circle in Maharashtra for 10 years.

The company's shares were over 3% in recent trade.

data.title: Volkswagen to Sponsor UEFA Women's European Championship 2025
data.description:
Volkswagen was named as an official partner for the UEFA Women's European Championship in Switzerland, according to a Thursday release.

The carmaker will also support seven participating national teams from Germany, Switzerland, France, the Netherlands, Denmark, Finland, and Italy under sponsorship agreements.

The event will run from July 2 to July 27.

data.title: Mangal Credit and Fincorp Opens New Branch in Thane, India
data.description:
Mangal Credit and Fincorp has opened a new branch at Thane in Maharashtra, India, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The company's shares were up nearly 2% in recent trade.
